topic: New topic: sharp partial identification for staggered-adoption DiD when adoption time is interval-censored rather than observed exactly. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Sun-Abraham and Callaway-Sant'Anna group-time ATT identification with known adoption cohorts, plus Honest-DiD sensitivity work; our theorem is not that because the latent adoption-date refinement is unobserved, so the target is the geometry of all event-time ATT paths compatible with coarsened cohort bins. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an endpoint certificate over latent timing refinements and a two-cohort witness where two refinements induce identical observed coarsened panels but opposite event-time contrast signs. Avoid merely reusing LP duality or pretrend sensitivity; if the contribution is only a missing-cell Manski bound, pivot.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp identified set for event-time ATT paths when exact staggered-adoption dates are latent refinements inside observed adoption-time intervals?

source_refs.claim: Group-time ATT identification and aggregation are built around known treatment cohorts in staggered DiD.
source_refs.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: Interaction-weighted event-study estimands remove TWFE contamination but require exact cohort/event-time indexing.
source_refs.author: Sun and Abraham

source_refs.claim: Staggered-adoption DiD with misclassification and anticipation studies biased standard estimators and recovery of average effects, not the geometry of latent event-time refinements.
source_refs.author: Augustin, Gutknecht, and Liu

source_refs.excerpt: observable distribution P=law of (Y_t, G, X) ... adoption cohort G in {1,...,T,infty}
sharpness depends on the variation-independent anticipation-box assumption

open_problems.why_unsolved: The known-cohort DiD papers identify CATT or group-time ATT objects after conditioning on the exact first-treatment cohort, while the closest local prior proposal varies anticipation distortions with the cohort label still observed. The newer misclassification-in-staggered-adoption literature is adjacent, but it targets biased observed/true switching estimators and misclassified treatment status rather than the full image of dynamic event-time ATT paths over all latent adoption-date refinements inside a coarsened cohort bin.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to treat each interval bin as a known cohort fails because the Track-A prior assumes an observed cohort label G and the Track-B event-study papers index effects by exact event time, while a single coarsened panel can relabel the same observed outcome changes into different event-time cells.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified-set formula for the event-time ATT path, represented by endpoint certificates optimizing over all feasible latent timing refinements and accompanied by a sharpness proof.

open_problems.open_problem: When is the sign of a coarsened-adoption event-time contrast invariant over all latent timing refinements, and when can two refinements reverse it?

source_refs.claim: Staggered TWFE DiD is a weighted average of 2x2 comparisons and can be biased when effects vary over time.
source_refs.author: Goodman-Bacon

source_refs.claim: TWFE coefficients can be non-convex and wrong-signed under heterogeneous effects even with observed treatment timing.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille

source_refs.excerpt: Replace the global Delta pair with lambda-targeted distortions
solve the sign-restricted LPs for one nontrivial signed A
baseline-pooling coarsening loses sign-relevant identifying information

open_problems.why_unsolved: The weight-decomposition literature diagnoses wrong signs and non-convex weighting when treatment timing is observed, and the failed CausalSmith pretrend proposal explicitly requested sign-restricted, lambda-targeted distortions for a known signed aggregation map. Neither source resolves the coarsened-timing question where the signed aggregation map itself ranges over latent cohort refinements and the same observed panel may support opposite event-time signs.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt of applying Goodman-Bacon or sign-restricted LP weights after expanding the bins fails because the expansion is unobserved and the prior reviewer notes that the relevant object is a lambda-targeted signed contrast, not a global bound on all distortions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient sign-identification certificate that either proves all feasible refinements share the sign of a specified event-time contrast or constructs two feasible refinements with identical observed coarsened panels and opposite signs.

open_problems.open_problem: How should Honest-DiD trend-violation sensitivity be combined with interval-censored adoption timing without treating timing nonidentification as an ordinary parallel-trends bias term?

source_refs.claim: Robust DiD inference partially identifies event-study causal parameters under restrictions on post-treatment violations of parallel trends relative to pre-trends.
source_refs.author: Rambachan and Roth

source_refs.claim: Bounded DiD variation motivates partial identification by restricting departures from parallel trends.
source_refs.author: Manski and Pepper

source_refs.excerpt: observed aggregate event-study vector is y=A delta
sharp cohort-level breakdown frontiers requires an additional quotient-rank condition
primitive active-face certificate ... is especially provisional

open_problems.why_unsolved: Honest-DiD and bounded-variation approaches form identified sets around a fixed event-study target or a fixed aggregation map. The prior Honest-DiD proposal already flags that sharp cohort-level frontiers require quotient-rank and active-face conditions even before timing is latent; interval-censored adoption adds a second layer in which the refinement-induced aggregation map A is itself unidentified.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt of adding a timing-error margin to a Rambachan-Roth sensitivity interval fails because the prior proposal models y=A delta with fixed A, whereas latent timing changes A and therefore changes the estimand before any trend-violation bias is added.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A joint sharp identified set over feasible latent timing refinements and admissible trend-violation paths, with conditions under which the set decomposes into a timing-refinement component and an Honest-DiD bias component.

open_problems.open_problem: Which primitive information about the adoption-time coarsening process point-identifies or sharply tightens bounds for dynamic staggered-adoption ATTs?

source_refs.claim: DiD with treatment observed in only one period can be point-identified using a proxy, stationarity of the propensity score, and an exclusion restriction.
source_refs.author: Botosaru and Gutierrez

source_refs.claim: Endogenous one-sided treatment misclassification can be corrected in a parametric DID regression with heterogeneous effects.
source_refs.author: Negi and Negi

source_refs.claim: ATT can be partially identified in DiD with missing pretreatment treatment status using copula bounds, IVs, or matched subsamples.
source_refs.author: Fan and Manzanares

source_refs.claim: Interval-measured regressors motivate identified regions rather than point estimates when the latent covariate is only known to lie in an interval.
source_refs.author: Manski and Tamer

open_problems.why_unsolved: The missing or mismeasured-treatment DiD papers obtain point identification or partial identification using proxies, stationarity restrictions, exclusion restrictions, parametric one-sided misclassification, copula restrictions, or matched subsamples. Those restrictions are not yet translated into a staggered-adoption setting where the latent variable is first-adoption time and where the estimand depends on exposure duration and event-time cell assignment rather than only ever-treated status.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt of borrowing a one-period proxy or interval-regressor correction fails because a proxy for treatment status or a bound on the latent covariate does not determine the whole adoption history, exposure length, or event-time cell receiving each observed outcome.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A hierarchy of coarsening assumptions, such as coarsening-at-random within bins, validation subsamples, known endpoint probabilities, or monotone timing errors, each paired with either a point-identification formula or a sharp bound-tightening theorem for dynamic group-time ATTs.

literature_map:
1. Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics) and Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics) are the closest known-cohort anchors; both identify dynamic or group-time ATT objects using exact treatment cohorts.
2. Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics) and de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) show observed-timing TWFE can mix heterogeneous effects with non-convex or wrong-sign weights.
3. Athey and Imbens (2022, Journal of Econometrics) give a design-based staggered-adoption framework indexed by adoption dates, again with the adoption date as an observed or assigned object.
4. Augustin, Gutknecht, and Liu (2025, arXiv) study staggered-adoption DiD with misclassification and anticipation, the closest current web anchor to timing ambiguity, but not interval-censored event-time path geometry.
5. Botosaru and Gutierrez (2018, Journal of Applied Econometrics), Fan and Manzanares (2017, Econometric Reviews), and Negi and Negi (2025, Journal of Applied Econometrics/arXiv record) study missing or misclassified treatment in DiD-like settings with proxies, restrictions, or parametric corrections.
6. Manski and Tamer (2002, Econometrica) is the classic interval-data anchor: latent variables known only inside intervals typically generate identified regions.
7. Rambachan and Roth (2023, Review of Economic Studies) and Manski and Pepper (2018, ReStat) supply partial-ID/sensitivity tools for violations of parallel trends, but they work around fixed treatment timing and fixed event-study targets.
8. Recent dynamic/intertemporal and continuous-treatment DiD work handles lags, doses, or nonbinary treatment paths, but still assumes observed treatment histories or observed timing/dose variables.
9. The literature gap is not merely missing cells: interval-censored adoption changes which observed outcomes load onto each event-time contrast.
10. The requested nonroutine object is therefore an endpoint certificate over latent timing refinements plus an observational-equivalence witness, not another TWFE decomposition, LP duality exercise, or Honest-DiD pretrend sensitivity interval.
11. The study-pipeline gaps on LATE and Manski/Tamer interval regression were reviewed as background; they are not emitted as standalone gaps because they do not target staggered-adoption event-time DiD.
12. Dominant routing is partial_id, with panel/exact_id substructure for sign-invariance and endpoint-certificate subproblems.
prior_proposal_map:
Inspected relevant active/archived proposal files, rejected-angle files, reviewer JSONs, and _bank READMEs under doc/formalization only.
Closest direct prior is pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1: sharp staggered-DID ATT bounds, but with exact cohort G and bounded anticipation, not latent adoption refinements.
Closest partial-ID geometry prior is pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1: known cohort/event aggregation and lambda-support gaps, including reviewer strengthening paths for signed LP contrasts, but no interval-censored timing.
Closest sensitivity prior is panel_honest_did_phase_v1: quotient-rank warnings for aggregate Honest-DiD frontiers, but fixed event grid and known aggregation map A.
Reusable warning comes from pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v1: coarsening can lose sign-relevant information, but generic finite latent-response LP sharpness is not enough.
Adjacent sign-witness prior is eid_cic_cl_quantile_nonequiv_v1: opposite-sign staggered timing idea, but failed for missing concrete non-corner witness and QTT-specific issues.
Panel matrix-completion review notes mention algorithmic identifiability and staggered-mask separability, but they are not emitted separately because the present topic needs timing-refinement endpoints rather than low-rank completion.
